title: Zoto Eval Non-Skill Eval File
description: Top-level wrapper schema for a non-skill `evals/<name>.json` file (commands / agents / hooks / central JSON files). The file declares the `target_id` once and an array of `cases` each conforming to `case.schema.json` (either a DeclarativeCase or a RunnerCase). Skill files use the Cursor-spec shape (`skill_name` + `evals[]`) and are out of scope for this schema — they continue to validate against the skill template only. Consumers MUST register `case.schema.json` and `case-meta.schema.json` with their Ajv instance before compiling this schema so the `$ref` chain resolves.

target_id.description: Stable identifier for the primitive under test. Mirrors the manifest `targets[].id` field. Skill targets are NOT permitted in this schema — skill evals stay in their own `evals.json` shape.

model_id.description: Optional override for the agent-under-test model id. Falls back to `ZOTO_EVAL_MODEL` env / `composer-2.5` at runtime.

judge_model.description: Optional override for the LLM judge model id. Falls back to `ZOTO_EVAL_JUDGE_MODEL` env / `opus-4.6` at runtime.

case_timeout_ms.description: Optional per-case Vitest timeout in milliseconds for light cases. Heavy cases automatically get a multiplied budget — see `LlmEvalConfig` in `evals/llm/_shared/run-llm-suite.ts`.

cases.description: One entry per eval case. Each case is either a DeclarativeCase or a RunnerCase as defined in `case.schema.json`.

_meta.description: Optional file-level metadata (e.g. `generated: true` for stamped files). Per-case `_meta` lives inside each case entry and remains the user-vs-generated discriminator at row granularity.
